response.justification: Bogdan is a seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine depth in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and developer tooling, but the core requirements of this role — Java and Kotlin expertise, deep JVM knowledge, Spring ecosystem experience, and IDE internals for JVM languages — are almost entirely absent from his profile. His one adjacent data point (C/C++ work on NI LabWindows/CVI, a C IDE) is relevant in spirit but wrong language and nearly a decade old. Nothing in his history suggests experience with Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or JVM build tooling (Gradle/Maven), which are the technical pillars of this position.
strengths: Genuine developer-tooling DNA: built test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial), MCP code generators, and contributed to a real IDE (NI LabWindows/CVI), showing authentic interest in the problem space
Broad systems experience across web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency microservices demonstrates adaptability and architectural range
Strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code kata facilitation) align with the quality and reliability standards expected at this seniority level
concerns: Java appears only in passing (robotic arms, mobile orchestration tooling at Altom) with no evidence of production-grade Java or Kotlin development, and Kotlin is entirely absent
No Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM enterprise framework experience visible anywhere in the resume
Currently in a career gap (since October 2025) with no indication of JVM skilling up during that period
